info.name: Tito Backend API
info.description:
Laravel 12 + Passport + WebAuthn + TFA.

Auth mode:

- Send X-Auth-Mode: token to receive tokens in JSON (default).
    
- Send X-Auth-Mode: cookie to receive HttpOnly cookies instead.
    

All successful JSON responses are wrapped in { data: ... } by WrapApiResponses.

query.description: Required. Entity type key. Allowed: lead, contact, company, property, vendor_company, project, tenant_user, email, phone, address, assignment, contact_assignment, file, file_folder, email_template, document_template
Required. Entity primary key (ULID, max 64 chars)
Boolean. When true (default), includes events where entity appears as a related entity, not just as the subject
Filter by event type. Operators: eq (default), ne, in, not_in, contains, starts_with, ends_with, does_not_contain. Example: property.updated
Filter by actor ID (ULID). Operators: eq (default), ne, in, not_in, contains, starts_with, ends_with, does_not_contain
Filter by origin. Values: api, system, job. Operators: eq (default), ne, in, not_in, contains, starts_with, ends_with, does_not_contain
Filter by request correlation ID (e.g., req_01jm...). Operators: eq (default), ne, in, not_in, contains, starts_with, ends_with, does_not_contain
Filter by workflow run ID (ULID). Operators: eq (default), ne, in, not_in, contains, starts_with, ends_with, does_not_contain
Date filter. Operators: eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, between (expects array of two ISO dates). Example: gte:2026-03-01T00:00:00Z
Free-text search across: event_type, subject_label, actor_label, related_label
Comma-separated signed fields (+ asc, - desc). Default: -occurred_at,-id
Page number (default: 1)
Items per page (default: 15, max: 200)

request.description:
List activity events for a specific entity, with optional filtering, search, and pagination.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`) and `activity` module view permission. Additionally, the viewer must have `view` permission on the subject entity.

**Required query params:**
- `filter[subject_type]`: one of the configured entity type keys
- `filter[subject_id]`: ULID of the target record

**Optional query params:**
- `filter[include_related]`: boolean (default true) — include events where entity is a related entity
- `filter[event_type]`, `filter[actor_id]`, `filter[origin]`, `filter[request_id]`, `filter[workflow_run_id]`: string filters
- `filter[occurred_at]`: date filter with operators
- `q`: free-text search
- `sort`: signed sort fields
- `page`, `per_page`: pagination

**String filter operators:** eq, ne, in, not_in/nin, contains, starts_with, ends_with, does_not_contain

**Date filter operators:** eq, ne, gt, gte, lt, lte, between

**Default sort:** `-occurred_at,-id`

**Authorization notes:**
- Module-level: viewer must have `activity` module view permission
- Subject-level: viewer must have `view` permission on the subject entity
- For commons entities (email, phone, address, file, assignment, contact_assignment), authorization is checked against the parent entity

**Event types follow pattern:** `{module}.{action}` (e.g., `property.created`, `contact.updated`, `email.deleted`, `email_template.cloned`)

**include parameter:** Prohibited — relations are always loaded.

**Example:**
- `?filter[subject_type]=property&filter[subject_id]=<ulid>&filter[event_type]=property.updated&sort=-occurred_at&page=1&per_page=25`

response.name: 200 Activity Events (Paginated)
422 Validation Error (Missing required filters)

request.description: Uses X-Auth-Mode (token|cookie). Cookie mode reads refresh token from cookies, sets HttpOnly cookies, and omits tokens from JSON.

Strict query DSL only. Legacy params `q`, `filters[]`, `sort[]`, `include[]`, top-level `page`, and `per_page` are not supported.

Available filters:
- `filter[search]`

This endpoint does not support `sort`, `include`, or `page[number]`/`page[size]`.
Example: `?filter[search]=user`

query.description: Full-text search across: name, guard_name, search_blob
Exact match, comma-separated for IN, !value for NOT, null/!null
Enum value. Supports: value, !value, value1,value2 for IN
Comma-separated fields. Prefix with - for DESC. Available: name
Comma-separated relationships to include. Available: permissions
Page number (default: 1)
Items per page (default: 15, max: 100)

request.description:
List Roles using canonical query DSL.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`).

**Query params (strict):**
- `filter[id]`, `filter[guard_name]`, `filter[search]`
- `sort`
- `include` (`permissions`)
- `page[number]`, `page[size]`

**Legacy params prohibited:** `q`, `filters`, `per_page`

**Sortable fields:**
- `name`

**Sort syntax:** comma-separated fields; prefix `-` for descending or use `field:asc|desc`

**Filter value operator syntax:**
- `value`, `!value`, `>value`, `>=value`, `<value`, `<=value`
- `a,b`, `!a,b`, `from..to`, `null`, `!null`

**Example:**
- `?filter[search]=abc&sort=-created_at&page[number]=1&page[size]=25`

Retrieve an entity profile picture as a binary image response.

The `entity_profile_picture_id` is the `id` returned in the `profile_picture` object on any entity that supports profile pictures (companies, contacts, leads, projects, vendor companies, vehicles, equipment, materials, resource types).

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`).

**Behavior:** response is a binary `image/webp` file, not wrapped in JSON.

Delete a single notification.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`).

**Path params:**
- `notification_id`: notification UUID

**Request body:** none

**Behavior:**
- Hard-deletes the notification.
- Returns 404 if the notification doesn't exist or belongs to another user.

query.description: Boolean. When true, returns only notifications where read_at is null.
Page number (default: 1)
Items per page (default: 25, max: 100)

request.description:
List the authenticated user's notifications, newest first.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`).

**Query params:**
- `filter[unread]` — boolean; when `true`, only unread notifications are returned
- `page[number]` — page number (default: 1)
- `page[size]` — items per page (default: 25, max: 100)

**Notes:**
- Results are scoped to the authenticated user (no other user's notifications are visible).
- Ordered by `created_at` descending.
- Returns standard Laravel pagination with `data`, `meta`, and `links`.

**Recommended on app start:**
- `GET /notifications?filter[unread]=true` to hydrate the notification bell.

response.name: 200 Notifications (Paginated)
422 Validation Error

request.description:
Delete multiple notifications in a single request.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`).

**Request body:**
- `ids` — required array of notification UUIDs (min: 1, max: 200, values must be distinct)

**Behavior:**
- Only deletes notifications that belong to the authenticated user.
- IDs that don't exist or belong to other users are silently ignored.
- Returns the count of notifications actually deleted.

response.name: 422 Validation Error

request.description:
Mark multiple notifications as read in a single request.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`).

**Request body:**
- `ids` — required array of notification UUIDs (min: 1, max: 200, values must be distinct)

**Behavior:**
- Only updates notifications that belong to the authenticated user and are currently unread.
- IDs that don't exist or belong to other users are silently ignored.
- Returns the count of notifications actually updated.

response.name: 422 Validation Error

request.description:
Mark a single notification as read.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`).

**Path params:**
- `notification_id`: notification UUID

**Request body:** none

**Behavior:**
- Sets `read_at` to the current timestamp if not already read.
- Idempotent — calling again on an already-read notification is a no-op.
- Returns 404 if the notification doesn't exist or belongs to another user.

request.description:
List Users using canonical query DSL.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`).

**Query params (strict):**
- `q`
- `filter[id]`, `filter[name]`, `filter[email]`, `filter[is_active]`, `filter[type_id]`, `filter[email_verified_at]`
- `filter[created_at]`, `filter[updated_at]`
- `sort`
- `include` (`roles`, `type`, `profilePicture`)
- `page[number]`, `page[size]`

**Sortable fields:**
- `name`, `email`, `is_active`, `email_verified_at`, `created_at`, `updated_at`

**Sort syntax:** comma-separated signed fields only (`+field` or `-field`).

**Filter value operator syntax:**
- `value`, `!value`, `>value`, `>=value`, `<value`, `<=value`
- `a,b`, `!a,b`, `from..to`, `empty()`, `!empty()`

**Profile visibility:**
- `type.resource_type_profile` is only populated for `type.module_type` in `equipment|vehicles|users`.

**Example:**
- `?q=john&sort=-created_at&page[number]=1&page[size]=25`

response.name: 200 Users (Paginated)

request.description:
Update a tenant user.

**Request body validation (supported fields):**
- `name`: sometimes|string|max:255
- `email`: sometimes|email|max:255
- `type_id`: sometimes|nullable|string|exists:types.id where module_type=`users`

**Note:**
- Proposal/resource defaults are no longer stored on users directly. Use `type.resource_type_profile` instead.
Ends the current tenant impersonation. Cookie mode clears tenant auth cookies.

originalRequest.description: Ends the current tenant impersonation. Cookie mode clears tenant auth cookies.

request.description:
Exchange a tenant impersonation token for tenant API authentication.

**Auth:** no prior tenant auth required; tenancy is resolved from path (`{{tenant_slug}}`).

**Path params:**
- `tenant_slug`
- `impersonation_token`

**Query params:** none

**Request body:** optional fallback
- `impersonation_token` may be sent in body, but path token is the canonical input

**Authentication mode:**
Uses `X-Auth-Mode` (`token`|`cookie`).
- **Token mode:** returns access + refresh token fields in JSON
- **Cookie mode:** sets tenant HttpOnly auth cookies and omits token fields from JSON

**Behavior:**
- Token is one-time/short-lived and must match tenant context
- Fails if target tenant user is inactive

response.name: 200 Impersonation Successful (Token Mode)

originalRequest.description:
Exchange a tenant impersonation token for tenant API authentication.

**Auth:** no prior tenant auth required; tenancy is resolved from path (`{{tenant_slug}}`).

**Path params:**

- `impersonation_token`

**Query params:** none

**Request body:** optional fallback
- `impersonation_token` may be sent in body, but path token is the canonical input

**Authentication mode:**
Uses `X-Auth-Mode` (`token`|`cookie`).
- **Token mode:** returns access + refresh token fields in JSON
- **Cookie mode:** sets tenant HttpOnly auth cookies and omits token fields from JSON

**Behavior:**
- Token is one-time/short-lived and must match tenant context
- Fails if target tenant user is inactive

response.body:
{
  "message": "Impersonation successful.",
  "data": {
    "user": {
      "id": "tenant_user_id",
      "name": "User Name",
      "email": "user@example.com",
      "profile_picture_url": null,
      "is_active": true,
      "type": {
        "id": "type_id",
        "name": "Foreman",
        "description": "Field supervisor",
        "module_type": "users",
        "is_active": true,
        "resource_type_profile": {
          "id": "resource_type_profile_id",
          "type_id": "type_id",
          "suggested_quantity": "2.0000",
          "minimum_quantity": "1.0000",
          "maximum_quantity": "4.0000",
          "suggested_rate": "55.0000",
          "rate_type": "HOUR",
          "created_at": "2026-02-22T12:00:00.000000Z",
          "updated_at": "2026-02-22T12:00:00.000000Z"
        },
        "created_at": "2026-01-01T00:00:00.000000Z",
        "updated_at": "2026-01-01T00:00:00.000000Z"
      },
      "roles": [],
      "permissions": {}
    },
    "tenant_slug": "{{tenant_slug}}",
    "redirect_url": "/dashboard",
    "access_token": "token",
    "refresh_token": "refresh_token",
    "expires_in": 3600,
    "token_type": "Bearer"
  }
}

response.name: 200 Impersonation Successful (Cookie Mode)

Strict query DSL only. Legacy params `q`, `filters[]`, `sort[]`, `include[]`, top-level `page`, and `per_page` are not supported.

Available filters:
- `filter[search]`

This endpoint does not support `sort`, `include`, or `page[number]`/`page[size]`.
Example: `?filter[search]=pending`

response.name: 409 Pending invitation conflict

request.description: Creates invitations in bulk. Emails are sent after successful transaction commit. Failures are reported per-email.

Delete a system column configuration by id.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`) and `system_configurations.delete` permission.

Fetch one system column configuration by id.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`) and `system_configurations.view` permission.

List system-level column configurations using canonical query DSL.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`) and `system_configurations.view` permission.

**Filterable fields:** `id`, `module`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
**Sortable fields:** `module`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
**Default sort:** `+module`
**Searchable (`q`):** none
**Includes:** none

Patch a system column configuration.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`), verified email, and `system_configurations.manage` permission.

**Body validation:**
- `data`: sometimes object/array

`module` is immutable on update.

Create a system-wide column configuration.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`), verified email, and `system_configurations.manage` permission.

**Body validation:**
- `module`: required enum (`ModuleType`)
- `data`: required object/array

Delete a tenant system configuration.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`), verified email, and `system_configurations.delete` permission.

Fetch a single tenant system configuration by id.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`) and `system_configurations.view` permission.

List tenant system configurations using canonical query DSL.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`) and `system_configurations.view` permission.

**Filterable fields:** `id`, `key`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
**Sortable fields:** `key`, `created_at`, `updated_at`
**Default sort:** `+key`
**Searchable (`q`):** none
**Includes:** none

response.name: 200 System Configurations

request.description:
List SES identities available to the tenant's current `aws_ses` configuration.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`) and `system_configurations.view` permission.

Use this endpoint to power sender/domain pickers in the UI with upstream SES domains and email identities, including domain coverage for exact parent domains only.

Coverage is intentionally conservative:
- `acme.com` covers `user@acme.com`
- `acme.com` does not cover `user@billing.acme.com`
- exact domain match only; subdomain inheritance is not implied

response.name: 200 Email Identities

request.description:
Patch a tenant system configuration.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`), verified email, and `system_configurations.manage` permission.

**Body validation:**
- `data`: sometimes nullable object/array
- `meta`: sometimes nullable object/array

`key` is immutable on update.

For `aws_ses`, when `data` is present you must provide:
- `aws_region`
- `from_email`
- `auth_type` of `assume_role` or `access_keys` when supplied
- exactly one auth strategy: (`role_arn` + `external_id`) or (`access_key_id` + `secret_access_key`)
- optional `ses_configuration_set`
- optional `meta.is_active` to activate tenant mail after configuration is ready.

For `allowed_email_addresses`, when `data` is present you must provide at least one `allowed_domains` or `allowed_emails` entry. Domains and emails are normalized to lowercase, activation only sticks after successful validation, and domain coverage is exact-match only: `acme.com` covers `user@acme.com` but not `user@billing.acme.com`.

Changing the `data` payload clears previous validation status in `meta`.

Create a tenant system configuration.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`), verified email, and `system_configurations.manage` permission.

**Body validation:**
- `key`: required snake_case string, unique
- `data`: nullable object/array
- `meta`: nullable object/array

Known key-specific validation currently applies to `aws_ses` and `allowed_email_addresses`. For `aws_ses` and `allowed_email_addresses`, operational state like `is_active` is stored in `meta`.

Run a live diagnostic check for a tenant system configuration.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`), verified email, and `system_configurations.manage` permission.

Currently the seeded `aws_ses` and `allowed_email_addresses` configurations support live testing.

The response tells the UI whether the configuration is ready, and returns detailed checks for AWS SES sendability, event tracking, or per-address SES identity coverage.

response.name: 200 AWS SES Test Result
200 Allowed Email Addresses Test Result

request.description:
Delete a user column configuration by id.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`).

**Ownership:** request is forbidden unless the configuration belongs to the authenticated user.

Fetch a user column configuration by id.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`).

**Ownership:** request is forbidden unless the configuration belongs to the authenticated user.

List current user's column configurations using canonical query DSL.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`).

The backend always forces `user_id` to the authenticated user, even if a different `filter[user_id]` is sent.

Patch a user column configuration.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`).

**Ownership:** request is forbidden unless the configuration belongs to the authenticated user.

**Body validation:**
- `data`: sometimes object/array

`module` is immutable on update.

Create a user-scoped column configuration for the authenticated user.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`).

**Body validation:**
- `module`: required enum (`ModuleType`)
- `data`: required object/array

`user_id` is always set server-side from the authenticated user.

Fetch a workspace branding asset from tenant context.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`).

**Path params:**
- `asset`: `logotype` or `logomark`

Update tenant workspace branding from tenant context.

**Body validation:**
- `logotype`: sometimes nullable image max 5MB
- `logomark`: sometimes nullable image max 5MB

item.name: Knowledge Bases
Categories

request.description: Obtiene las categorías organizadas de una KB específica.
Crea una categoría o subcategoría (usando parent_id).

item.description: Organización jerárquica de los documentos dentro de una KB.

item.name: Documents

request.description:
Crea un documento. Al hacerlo, el sistema crea automáticamente:
1. Metadatos del documento.
2. La versión inicial (v1).
3. El embedding vectorial para búsqueda RAG.
Actualiza el documento. Si se incluye contenido nuevo, el sistema crea una nueva versión histórica y recalcula los embeddings.

item.description: Gestión de documentos versionados y vectorizados.

request.description: Obtiene la lista de todas las bases de conocimiento configuradas.
Crea un nuevo contenedor de conocimiento (KB).

item.description: Gestión de contenedores de conocimiento (KB) y sus jerarquías.

item.name: Agents

request.description: Devuelve una colección paginada de todos los agentes del tenant.
Crea un nuevo agente configurando sus parámetros de arquitectura, comportamiento (brain) y capacidades.
Registra fragmentos de texto de la transcripción de la sesión.
Endpoint polimórfico para subir archivos de audio (WAV/MP3). Se almacena en la tabla `files` y se vincula a la sesión.
Inicia una nueva sesión de interacción. Retorna un `session_id` que se usará para registrar transcripciones y subir audios.

item.description: Gestión de agentes inteligentes de IA.

request.description:
Confirm the authenticated user's password to unlock sensitive operations (for example, TFA/passkeys) for a short window.

**Auth:** requires authenticated central session/token (`auth:central-api`).

**Query params:** none

**Request body validation:**
- `password`: required|string

**Behavior:**
- On success, marks password as recently confirmed (challenge passed)
- Requires verified email before allowing the challenge

response.name: 403 Email Verification Required
413 Payload Too Large

request.description:
Authenticate a central user with email and password.

**Query params:** none

**Request body:**
- `email` (required)
- `password` (required)
- `device_name` (optional)

**Authentication Mode:**
Uses `X-Auth-Mode` header (`token` or `cookie`).
- **Token mode:** returns `access_token` + `refresh_token` in JSON
- **Cookie mode:** sets HttpOnly central auth cookies and omits token fields from JSON while still returning `expires_in`

**Behavior:**
- If the account has 2FA enabled, returns `tfa_required=true` with a temporary `tfa_token` and no auth tokens
- If the account email is unverified, successful responses include `email_verification_required=true` and may include `verification_sent`
- Validation and credential errors return `VALIDATION_ERROR` with `source.pointer` set to `_form`

response.name: 200 Authenticated (Cookie Mode)
200 Authenticated (Email Verification Required)
200 Authenticated (Cookie Mode + Email Verification Required)
200 TFA Required
200 TFA Required (Email Verification Required)
422 Validation Error (_form pointer)
413 Payload Too Large

request.description:
Log out the currently authenticated central user.

**Auth:** requires authenticated central session/token (`auth:central-api`).

**Query params:** none

**Request body:** none

**Behavior:**
- Revokes the current access token and its linked refresh token
- In cookie mode, also clears central auth cookies
- Returns success message even when token state is already effectively logged out

response.name: 413 Payload Too Large

request.description:
Refresh central auth tokens for the authenticated user.

**Auth:** requires authenticated central session/token (`auth:central-api`).

**Query params:** none

**Request body validation:**
- `refresh_token`: required|string
  - in cookie mode, this is auto-read from the central refresh cookie if omitted

**Authentication mode:**
Uses `X-Auth-Mode` (`token`|`cookie`).
- **Token mode:** returns refreshed `access_token` + `refresh_token` + `token_type` + `expires_in` in JSON
- **Cookie mode:** refreshes HttpOnly cookies and omits token fields from JSON (`expires_in` only)

**Behavior:**
- Endpoint is rate-limited (`throttle:auth.refresh`)

response.name: 422 Missing Refresh Token
413 Payload Too Large

request.description:
Create a new central user account.

**Query params:** none

**Request body validation:**
- `name`: required|string|max:255
- `email`: required|string|email|max:255|unique:users,email
  - input is normalized before validation: `trim + lowercase`
- `password`: required|string|Password::defaults()|confirmed
- `password_confirmation`: required when sending `password` (for `confirmed`)
- `device_name`: sometimes|nullable|string|max:255

**Authentication mode:**
Uses `X-Auth-Mode` (`token`|`cookie`) for consistency with auth endpoints. Register does not issue auth tokens in either mode.

**Behavior:**
- Returns `201 Created` on success
- `email_verification_required` is always `true`
- `verification_sent` indicates if a verification email was sent in this request

response.name: 201 Registered (Verification Sent)
201 Registered (Verification Not Sent)
422 Validation Error
422 Email Already Taken
413 Payload Too Large

request.description:
Complete login for a user with two-factor authentication enabled.

**Auth:** not required.

**Query params:** none

**Request body validation:**
- `tfa_token`: required|string
- `code`: required|string|min:4|max:64
- `device_name`: sometimes|nullable|string|max:255

**Authentication mode:**
Uses `X-Auth-Mode` (`token`|`cookie`).
- **Token mode:** returns `access_token` + `refresh_token` in JSON
- **Cookie mode:** sets HttpOnly central auth cookies and omits token fields from JSON while still returning `expires_in`

**Behavior:**
- Accepts a current TOTP code or an unused recovery code
- TFA session must be valid (cached `tfa_token`)
- Invalid code attempts are limited; after max attempts the session is invalidated

response.name: 200 Authenticated (Token Mode)
200 Authenticated (Cookie Mode)
422 Validation Error
422 Invalid TFA Code
429 Too Many Invalid Codes
413 Payload Too Large

request.description:
Verify a central user's email address using a signed verification URL.

**Auth:** requires authenticated central session/token (`auth:central-api`).

**Path params:**
- `verification_id`: central user id
- `verification_hash`: `sha1(user_email)`

**Query params:**
- `signature` (required): signed URL signature
- `expires` (required): signed URL expiration timestamp

**Behavior:**
- Validates the signed URL (`signed` middleware)
- Loads the user by `verification_id`
- Validates `verification_hash` against the user's email
- Marks email as verified and dispatches verification event on success
- Returns validation errors when the hash is invalid or email is already verified

response.name: 403 Invalid Signature
422 Already Verified

request.description:
Request a password reset link.

**Auth:** not required.

**Query params:** none

**Request body validation:**
- `email`: required|string|email
  - input is normalized before validation: `trim + lowercase`

**Behavior:**
- Always returns the same success message, regardless of whether the email exists
- Endpoint is rate-limited (`throttle:auth.forgot_password`)

response.name: 200 Email Sent
413 Payload Too Large

request.description:
Reset a central user's password with a valid reset token.

**Auth:** not required.

**Query params:** none

**Request body validation:**
- `email`: required|string|email
  - input is normalized before validation: `trim + lowercase`
- `token`: required|string
- `password`: required|string|Password::defaults()|confirmed
- `password_confirmation`: required when sending `password` (for `confirmed`)

**Behavior:**
- On success, updates password and revokes all existing central Passport access/refresh tokens for that user
- Returns `422 VALIDATION_ERROR` when reset broker validation fails (e.g. invalid/expired token)

response.name: 422 Validation Error
422 Invalid or Expired Token
413 Payload Too Large

request.description:
Resend a verification email for the authenticated central user.

**Auth:** requires authenticated central session/token (`auth:central-api`).

**Query params:** none

**Request body validation:**
- `reason`: sometimes|string|in:signup,update
  - defaults to `signup` when omitted

**Behavior:**
- If user is already verified, returns `422 VALIDATION_ERROR`
- Otherwise sends a new verification link and returns a success message
- Endpoint is rate-limited (`throttle:auth.email_verification`)

response.name: 200 Notification Sent
422 Already Verified
413 Payload Too Large

request.description:

**Auth:** requires authenticated central session/token (`auth:central-api`).

**Query params:** none

**Behavior:**
- Requires verified email
- Requires recent password confirmation (`POST /api/auth/tfa/challenge`)

request.description:
Confirm the authenticated user's password before sensitive 2FA actions.

**Auth:** requires authenticated central session/token (`auth:central-api`).

**Request body validation:**
- `password`: required|string

**Behavior:**

- Required before enable/confirm/recovery-codes/regenerate/disable operations

response.name: 403 Email Verification Required

request.description:

request.description:

**Auth:** requires authenticated central session/token (`auth:central-api`).

response.name: 403 Email Verification Required

Uses `X-Auth-Mode` (`token`|`cookie`).
- **Token mode:** returns access/refresh tokens in JSON

request.description: This endpoint does not expose filter parameters. It always returns the current user's active personal access tokens.

List pending invitations for the authenticated central user email.

**Auth:** requires authenticated central session/token (`auth:central-api`).

**Query params (strict):**
- `filter[search]`: nullable|string|max:255

**Prohibited params:** `q`, `sort`, `include`, `page`, `per_page`

**Behavior:**
- Returns only pending invitations addressed to current user email
- Expired invitations are marked and excluded from results

response.name: 413 Payload Too Large

request.description:
Resolve an invitation URL and fetch invitation details.

**Auth:** not required.

**Query params:**
- `expires` (required for signed middleware)
- `token` (required|string)
- `signature` (required for signed middleware)

**Important:** keep query param order as `expires`, `token`, `signature`; signature depends on original URL order.

**Behavior:**
- Signed middleware validates URL signature and expiry
- `token` is hashed server-side and matched against stored invitation token hash
- Stale or superseded links return `410 INVITATION_LINK_INVALID`
- Returns invitation resource including linked tenant

response.name: 403 Invalid Signature
410 Stale Invitation Link
413 Payload Too Large

request.description:
Accept a workspace invitation for the authenticated central user.

**Auth:** requires authenticated central session/token (`auth:central-api`).

**Path params:**
- `invitation_id`: invitation ULID

**Query params:** none

**Request body:** none

**Behavior:**
- Invitation email must match current user email
- Invitation must still be `pending`
- Attaches user to tenant and assigns default tenant role `user` only if user has no tenant roles

response.name: 403 Invitation Not Yours
422 Invitation No Longer Valid
413 Payload Too Large

request.description:

- `invitation_id`: invitation ULID

**Behavior:**

query.description: Full-text search across: name, slug, search_blob
Exact match, comma-separated for IN, !value for NOT, null/!null
Comma-separated fields. Prefix with - for DESC. Available: name, slug
Comma-separated relationships to include. Available: users
Page number (default: 1)
Items per page (default: 15, max: 100)

request.description:
List Tenants using canonical query DSL.

**Auth:** requires authenticated tenant session/token (`auth:tenant-api`).

**Query params (strict):**
- `filter[id]`, `filter[search]`
- `sort`
- `include` (`users`)
- `page[number]`, `page[size]`

**Legacy params prohibited:** `q`, `filters`, `per_page`

**Sortable fields:**
- `name`, `slug`

**Sort syntax:** comma-separated fields; prefix `-` for descending or use `field:asc|desc`

**Filter value operator syntax:**
- `value`, `!value`, `>value`, `>=value`, `<value`, `<=value`
- `a,b`, `!a,b`, `from..to`, `null`, `!null`

**Example:**
- `?filter[search]=abc&sort=-created_at&page[number]=1&page[size]=25`

response.name: 413 Payload Too Large

request.description:

- `slug`: sometimes nullable string max 255
- `logotype`: sometimes nullable image max 5MB
- `logomark`: sometimes nullable image max 5MB
- `remove_logotype`: sometimes boolean
- `remove_logomark`: sometimes boolean

Create a new tenant (workspace) for the authenticated central user.

**Auth:** requires authenticated central session/token (`auth:central-api`).

**Query params:** none

**Request body validation:**
- `name`: required|string|max:255
- `slug`: nullable|string|max:255

**Behavior:**
- Requires verified email to manage workspaces
- If `slug` is omitted, one is derived from name
- Slug is normalized and uniqueness is enforced (suffixes may be added)
- The current user is attached to the new tenant

response.name: 403 Email Verification Required
413 Payload Too Large

request.description:
Create a tenant impersonation URL for a workspace the authenticated central user can access.

**Auth:** requires authenticated central session/token (`auth:central-api`).

**Query params:** none

**Request body validation:**
- `tenant`: required|string|max:255 (tenant slug)
- `redirect_url`: sometimes|string|max:2048|starts_with:/

**Behavior:**
- Requires either direct tenant membership or `support`/`super_admin` role
- Requires mapped active tenant account for the central user
- Returns a tenant API impersonation URL and token

response.name: 403 Tenant Impersonation Unavailable
422 Validation Error
